calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Apportionment Proceeding And For An Order To Distribute Settlement Funds
Off Calendar Jul-31-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Notice Of Motion And Motion For Apportionment Proceeding And For An Order To Distribute Settlement Funds.
Dept. 514, Judge: Donald Sullivan, Clerk: Mars Goodman and Shawna Van Tree, CSR: Sonia Wong, #11288.

Amir Sarreshtehdary, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, Rober Threatt.
Beatrice Threatt, Raymond Threatt, Vince Threatt, Chante Threatt appearing on their own behalf.

The Court, having reviewed the moving papers and having considered the evidence, both oral and documentary, and good cause appearing, it is ordered that:

1. The following are entitled to distribution of all net funds derived from the personal injury-survival and wrongful death cause of action resulting from settlements negotiated after the February 24, 2008 death of Plaintiff Ronald Threatt, in the following proportions:

Beatrice Threatt - 40%
Raymond Threatt - 10%
Sherry Threatt - 10%
Vince Threatt - 10%
Cornell Threatt - 10%
Dominic Threatt - 10%
Estate of Ronald Threatt - 10%
Estate of Robert Threatt - 0%

2. Brayton Purcell LLP shall recover the net settlement funds of $3,475.71 currently in BRayton Purcell LLP's possesson in the sum of $1,500.00 as reasonable attorney's fees and costs incurred in this proceeding, leaving the resulting net amount of $1,975.71. Chante Threatt shall pay $500 of the costs. The balance of $1,000 in costs shall be equally divided by the other heirs.

3. The balance of the funds remaining after deduction of reasonable attorney's fees and costs incurred in this proceeding shall be deliverd by Brayton Purcell to the above persons,in the percentages set forth in paragraph 1 hereinabove.

4. Any net fundsrecovered in the future that are derived from settlements made after February 24, 2008 death of Plaintiff Roland Threatt, after costs and attorney's fees, shall be distributed in the percentages set forth in paragraph 1 hereinabove.

4. The signature of Beatrice Threatt shall be sufficient on all settlement documents for the purpose of settling both the estate claim and the wrongful death claims with defendants in the filed asbestos personal injury-survival and wrongful death action and in those claims made against bankruptcy-related trusts established for asbestos victims.

Judgment signed in open court.
